Class BitcoinReporter

java.lang.Object
dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.reporter.Reporter
dom.institution.lab.cns.bitcoin.reporter.BitcoinReporter
public class BitcoinReporter extends dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.reporter.Reporter
Specialized reporter for Bitcoin simulations extending the base Reporter.

In addition to the general simulation reports produced by Reporter, BitcoinReporter provides detailed reporting on:

  • The state of the blockchain (including sequences of block IDs and orphaned blocks).
  • Individual block events such as validation, mining, and propagation.

These reports are intended to be used from within Bitcoin-specific simulation entities, e.g., BitcoinNode or Block.

See Also:
  • Reporter
  • BitcoinNode
  • Block

  • Constructor Details

    • BitcoinReporter

      public BitcoinReporter()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public static void initialize()
      Initializes reporting settings from simulation configuration properties.

      Sets reportBlockEvents, reportStructureEvents, and reportAttackEvents based on the configuration keys reporter.reportBlockEvents, reporter.reportStructureEvents, and reporter.reportAttackEvents, respectively.

    • reportBlockChainState

      public static void reportBlockChainState(String[] blockchain, String[] orphans)
      Adds blockchain and orphan block state to the structure log.
      Parameters:
      blockchain - an array of strings representing the current blockchain state
      orphans - an array of strings representing the orphaned blocks

    • reportBlockEvent

      public static void reportBlockEvent(int simID, long simTime, long sysTime, int nodeID, int blockID, int parentID, int height, String txInvolved, String blockEvt, double difficulty, double cycles)
      Adds a detailed event line for a single block to the block log.

      This includes simulation IDs, node and block IDs, height, involved transactions, event type, validation difficulty, and mining cycles used.

      Parameters:
      simID - the simulation ID
      simTime - the simulation time of the event
      sysTime - the system time of the event
      nodeID - the ID of the node relating to the event
      blockID - the ID of the block
      parentID - the parent block ID (or -1 if none)
      height - the height of the block in the chain
      txInvolved - a string listing transactions included in the block
      blockEvt - a description of the block event (e.g., "Node Complete Validation")
      difficulty - the mining difficulty under which the block was validated
      cycles - the number of cycles dedicated to validate the block

    • reportAttackEvent

      public static void reportAttackEvent(int simId, long simTime, long sysTime, int nodeId, String eventType, int txId, int blockId, int blockHeight, int hiddenChainLength, int publicChainLength, String description)
      Reports an attack-related event to the attack log.

      This method logs significant attack events such as:

      • Target transaction arrival
      • Attack initiation
      • Hidden chain block mining
      • Chain revelation
      Parameters:
      simId - simulation ID
      simTime - simulation time when event occurred
      sysTime - system time when event occurred
      nodeId - ID of the node involved in the event
      eventType - type of attack event (e.g., "Target TX Arrival", "Attack Start", "Chain Reveal")
      txId - transaction ID (if applicable, -1 otherwise)
      blockId - block ID (if applicable, -1 otherwise)
      blockHeight - height of the block in the chain (if applicable, -1 otherwise)
      hiddenChainLength - current length of the attacker's hidden chain
      publicChainLength - current length of the public chain
      description - additional description of the event

    • flushCustomReports

      public static void flushCustomReports()
      Flushes all custom Bitcoin reports (block events, structure, and attack events) to files.

    • flushAll

      public static final void flushAll()

    • flushBlockReport

      public static void flushBlockReport()
      Save Block report to file. File name is "BlockLog - [Simulation Date Time].csv"

    • flushStructReport

      public static void flushStructReport()
      Save Blockchain report to file. File name is "StructureLog - [Simulation Date Time].csv"

    • flushAttackReport

      public static void flushAttackReport()
      Save Attack report to file. File name is "AttackLog - [Simulation Date Time].csv"

    • reportBlockEvents

      public static void reportBlockEvents(boolean reportBlockEvents)
      Sets whether block-level events should be logged.
      Parameters:
      reportBlockEvents - true to enable block event reporting, false to disable

    • reportStructureEvents

      public static void reportStructureEvents(boolean reportStructureEvents)
      Sets whether blockchain structure events should be logged.
      Parameters:
      reportStructureEvents - true to enable structure event reporting, false to disable

    • reportAttackEvents

      public static void reportAttackEvents(boolean reportAttackEvents)
      Sets whether attack events should be logged.
      Parameters:
      reportAttackEvents - true to enable attack event reporting, false to disable

    • reportAttackDetails

      public static void reportAttackDetails(boolean reportAttackDetails)

    • reportsBlockEvents

      public static boolean reportsBlockEvents()
      Returns whether block-level events are being logged.
      Returns:
      true if block event reporting is enabled, false otherwise

    • reportsStructureEvents

      public static boolean reportsStructureEvents()
      Returns whether blockchain structure events are being logged.
      Returns:
      true if structure event reporting is enabled, false otherwise

    • reportsAttackEvents

      public static boolean reportsAttackEvents()
      Returns whether attack events are being logged.
      Returns:
      true if attack event reporting is enabled, false otherwise
